activeAlerts.alert: Landslip affecting beach WEST of Mushroom Reef — do not approach. Toilet at Flinders Ocean Beach permanently closed.

PAPP poison baiting and trapping active from Portsea Ocean Beach to Greens Bush until October 2026. Lethal to dogs. Antidote (methylene blue) available — consult a vet immediately if pet suspected of ingestion.
Parks Victoria completing upgrades: resurfacing tracks, upgrading lookouts, improving boardwalk. Sections may be temporarily closed — follow on-site signage. Expected complete late April 2026.
No walk-specific closure listed, but secondary track closures possible due to fox control activity. Call Parks Victoria 13 1963 before setting out.

entities.name: Sorrento Back Beach (Sorrento Ocean Beach)
entities.dogNote: Dogs not permitted — Mornington Peninsula National Park
entities.accessibility: 40m accessible beach ramp (1:12 then 1:14) near kiosk — best ocean-beach accessibility in MPNP

entities.name: Portsea Back Beach (Portsea Ocean Beach)
entities.reviewNote: TripAdvisor claim re: rescue frequency — unverified

entities.name: Gunnamatta Ocean Beach
entities.notes: Most popular surf beach in MPNP; most hazardous for swimming (VMP). Best conditions April (offshore winds + groundswells).

entities.name: Rye Ocean Beach
entities.notes: Swimming not advised (Parks Victoria). Numerous reef breaks, strong rips and currents.

entities.name: Flinders Ocean Beach (Mushroom Reef)
entities.activeAlert: Landslip west of Mushroom Reef. Do not approach. Toilet permanently closed.
entities.notes: Mushroom Reef Marine Sanctuary — 80ha, basalt reef, weedy seadragons. Snorkelling depth 1–3m, best at low to mid tide.
entities.reviewNote: Verify landslip status before each publish

entities.name: Sorrento Front Beach
entities.dogNote: Standard Shire rule applies — leash required; prohibited from sand 9am–7pm during daylight saving
entities.accessibility: Beach matting installed Nov–Apr (near The Baths)

entities.name: Portsea Front Beach
entities.notes: Scuba diving hub; historic jetties and boat sheds. Portsea pier snorkelling — weedy seadragons.

entities.name: Mornington Main (Mothers Beach)
entities.notes: Protected by pier positioning — generally flat and calm. Schnapper Point Drive Mornington.

entities.name: Mount Martha Beach
entities.accessibility: Beach matting daily Nov–May at SLSC; two MobiChairs (floats) weekends during patrol 12–5pm. Phone (03) 5974 4140.
entities.notes: 2km beach divided by Balcombe Creek. Southern end recommended (Mt Martha South, 1km).

entities.name: Safety Beach
entities.notes: Tassells Cove nearby — year-round off-leash. Calm and shallow; good for paddleboarding.

entities.name: Rosebud Beach
entities.accessibility: Beach matting Nov–May at Rosebud Jetty (daily)
entities.notes: Shallow; calm; extensive foreshore parklands, BBQs, playgrounds.

entities.name: Rye Front Beach
entities.notes: Rye Pier snorkelling — warm waters, 6m depth near pier. Safe and shallow bay.

entities.name: Balnarring Beach
entities.notes: Tidal flats. 'Australia's Cleanest Beach' claim from one reviewer — unverified against KAB Council.

entities.name: Flinders Beach (Western Port north end)
entities.dogNote: Off-leash year-round between Dodds Creek and Manton Creek (north end, 3km stretch)
entities.notes: Distinct from Flinders Ocean Beach / Mushroom Reef (ocean-facing).

entities.name: Bushrangers Bay
entities.notes: Unpredictable rips, strong currents, rocky reefs, large waves — swimming not recommended. Limited emergency service access. Hooded Plover nesting on dunes.
entities.reviewNote: Verify walk access status — possible fox baiting secondary closures

entities.name: Two Bays Walking Track

walk.distanceNote: Parks Victoria official 26km. Third-party sources (Trail Hiking AU, Two Bays Trail Run) cite 28km — likely different measurement endpoints. Use 26km as primary.
walk.direction: North to south (Dromana → Cape Schanck) recommended

camping.name: Lightwood Creek Camping Area
camping.facilities: pit toilet, tank water (boil before drinking)
camping.bookings: No fees, no bookings — call 13 1963 to check availability (education groups sometimes occupy)

walk.wayfinding: Blue Wren circular symbol; orange markers = through tracks; blue markers = circuit walks

entities.reviewNote: Flag 26km vs 28km discrepancy in all content

entities.name: Cape Schanck Boardwalk
entities.activeAlert: Parks Victoria upgrades in progress. Expected complete late April 2026. Follow on-site signage.
entities.notes: Cape Schanck Lighthouse built 1859, heritage listed. Guided tours weekends + holidays. Kiosk open weekends/holidays.
entities.reviewNote: Verify if boardwalk works now complete

entities.name: Point Nepean National Park
entities.accessibility: Quarantine Station, Coles Track, Defence Road accessible; shuttle accommodates standard (not motorised) wheelchair; two all-terrain beach wheelchairs + one standard wheelchair FREE from Information Centre
entities.swimmingNote: Bay beach and Quarantine Station swimming permitted with care (strong currents/rips). Ocean beach access from shore PROHIBITED.

entities.name: Greens Bush
entities.notes: Largest remnant native bushland on the peninsula, between Arthurs Seat and Cape Schanck. Eucalypt forest, grass trees, tree ferns, kangaroos. Phone reception unreliable — carry printed map.

walk.twoBaysSection: 8.9km of Two Bays Walking Track passes through Greens Bush

entities.name: Mushroom Reef Marine Sanctuary
entities.notes: 80ha marine sanctuary at Flinders; basalt reef (ancient lava flows); weedy seadragons, seastars, anemones. 100+ years scientific study. Unique Black-and-white Sea Star found only on basalt reefs in Vic/Tas.
entities.activeAlert: Landslip west of Mushroom Reef — do not approach. Toilet permanently closed.

snorkelling.bestConditions: Low to mid tide (calm water); north/east wind preferred

entities.reviewNote: Verify landslip status before each publish

entities.name: Dog beaches — year-round off-leash
offLeashYearRound: Somers Beach (east of yacht club — with some time-of-day variation)
Flinders Beach north end (between Dodds Creek and Manton Creek)
entities.reviewNote: Verify current full Shire list each season

entities.name: Dog beaches — Shire standard rule
entities.rule: Dogs allowed on leash at all times on Shire-managed beaches. During daylight saving: prohibited from sand 9am–7pm. Some beaches have additional Purple/Yellow/Green zone restrictions.
entities.reviewNote: Verify current Shire rules URL and zone map each season

entities.name: Accessible beaches — beach matting program
entities.mobiChair: Two floating MobiChairs at Mount Martha SLSC — weekends during patrol 12–5pm. Phone (03) 5974 4140.
entities.reviewNote: Verify current season list with MPS Shire
